item.title: Beko Cassinetta: overcoming the crisis, results matter now
item.description: One year after the agreement that saved the Beko plant in Cassinetta, the first results and future challenges emerge
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Beko Cassinetta has overcome the critical phase
- Investments activated: 110 million euros
- Short-time work still in use

## Key facts
- **What**: Monitoring of Beko's industrial plan
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy
- Who: Honourable Antonio Ferrara (M5S)
- Amount: 110 million euros of activated investments

A year after the agreement that prevented the closure of Beko's plants, the deputy from Varese, Antonio Ferrara, attended the monitoring of the industrial plan at the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy. Ferrara, a member of the Productive Activities Commission, commented: 'Being present at this table means verifying in detail what is happening. After a year, it is fair to recognize that the most critical phase has been overcome, but today we are in the decisive phase: that of concrete results.'

Regarding Cassinetta di Biandronno, a clear picture emerges: the reorganization has been carried out, but the full phase of industrial consolidation has not yet been reached. Ferrara continued: 'In Cassinetta, we have intervened on the organization and personnel, but the subsequent phase, that of investments which must stabilize production and employment, is not yet fully visible. Volumes are not growing significantly and the use of short-time work continues, which should instead progressively decrease.'

To date, approximately 110 million euros of investments have been activated, but the overall plan envisages a much broader commitment. Ferrara emphasized: 'The point is not only how much is invested, but when these investments arrive. Timing is crucial: without acceleration, the risk is that the plan will lose effectiveness.'

In summary, a year after the agreement, the reduction of personnel has been achieved while the industrial relaunch is still partial. Volumes and turnover are substantially in line with those of the previous year, a sign that the expected leap in quality has not yet occurred. Particular attention has also been paid to the situation of the workers and technicians involved in the reorganization processes. Ferrara emphasized: 'This is a fundamental component of the company. Transparency and correctness must be guaranteed in employment paths, especially in such a delicate phase of change. No category should be left on the margins.'

### Implications for workers

Monitoring only makes sense if it leads to concrete results. After a year, we are in the verification phase: investments, production, and employment must go in the planned direction. The Five Star Movement will continue to be present, at the tables and in the institutions, to ensure that the commitments made are respected. Cassinetta remains a central point, and on this, we will not lower our guard. - ## Analysis of the current situation

The current situation of the Beko plant in Cassinetta di Biandronno is complex and requires an in-depth analysis. Although the critical phase has been overcome, concrete results are still far off. The investments activated amount to approximately 110 million euros, but the overall plan envisages a much broader commitment. This means that, although significant steps forward have been made, there is still a lot of work to be done to achieve the set objectives.

### Investments and timelines

One of the key points highlighted by Ferrara concerns the timelines of the investments. It is crucial that these arrive promptly to avoid the plan losing effectiveness. Without acceleration, the risk is that the objectives will not be achieved within the expected times. This could have negative repercussions not only on production but also on employment and the stability of the plant.

### Impact on production and employment

Production and employment are two closely related aspects. The reduction of personnel has been implemented, but the industrial relaunch is still partial. Volumes and turnover are substantially in line with those of the previous year, a sign that the expected quality leap has not yet occurred. This means that, despite the efforts, there is still a lot to do to improve the situation.

### Situation of the workers

The situation of the workers employed and technicians is another fundamental aspect. These workers are involved in the reorganization processes and must be guaranteed transparency and correctness in the employment paths. Ferrara underlined the importance of not leaving any category on the margins, especially in such a delicate phase of change.

### Future prospects

The future prospects for the Beko plant in Cassinetta di Biandronno depend greatly on the investments and the timelines. It is fundamental that the industrial plan is implemented quickly and that the objectives are achieved within the expected times. Only in this way can the stability of the plant be guaranteed and the current situation improved.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For workers and the company, it is crucial to act quickly to ensure that investments arrive promptly and that the industrial plan is implemented correctly. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken:

### Continuous monitoring

It is important to continue monitoring the situation and verifying that investments arrive on schedule. This can be done through monitoring tables and periodic meetings with stakeholders. Ferrara emphasized the importance of ensuring transparency and fairness in employment paths, especially during such a delicate phase of change.

### Ensuring transparency and fairness

It is fundamental to ensure transparency and fairness in employment paths. This means that workers must be promptly informed of any changes and their rights must be guaranteed. Ferrara emphasized the importance of not leaving any category behind, especially during such a delicate phase of change.

### Implementation of the industrial plan

The industrial plan must be implemented quickly and correctly. This means that investments must arrive promptly and that objectives must be achieved within the scheduled times. Only in this way can the stability of the plant be guaranteed and the current situation improved.

### Involvement of stakeholders

It is important to involve all stakeholders in the reorganization process. This means that workers, unions, and institutions must be actively involved and their rights guaranteed. Ferrara emphasized the importance of ensuring transparency and fairness in employment paths, especially during such a delicate phase of change.

### Use of support tools

To help workers and the company navigate this phase of change, support tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used. This tool can help calculate net salary and better understand the financial implications of the decisions made.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/beko-a-cassinetta-il-commento-dopo-un-anno-superata-la-crisi-ora-contano-i-risultati/2561353/)*

item.title: Falling birth rates in Ticino: Deputy Isabella demands action
item.description: Claudio Isabella of the Centre criticises the lack of effective measures against the decline in births in the Canton of Ticino
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Deputy Claudio Isabella criticizes parliament's action
- Only one proposal approved out of several presented
- Lack of foresight in policies

## Key facts
- **What**: Criticism of inaction against declining birth rates
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: Deputy Claudio Isabella (Centre)
- Amount: Not specified

Deputy Claudio Isabella of the Centre has criticized the Ticino parliament after it approved only one of the proposals aimed at combating the decline in births. Isabella strongly criticized the lack of foresight in Canton Ticino's policies regarding low birth rates. According to the deputy, it is not enough to recognize the problem; concrete action with effective measures is necessary.

### The current situation
Declining birth rates are an increasing problem in Ticino, with a constant drop in births over the past years. The parliament approved only one of the proposals presented to address this demographic crisis. Isabella expressed concern over the lack of concrete initiatives and called for greater commitment from the cantonal authorities.

### Isabella's criticisms
Claudio Isabella emphasized that declining birth rates are not a problem that can be solved with generic statements. A concrete action plan with specific measures to encourage births and support families is necessary. The deputy also criticized the lack of coordination among the different proposals presented, which has led to a stalemate.

### Approved proposals
Among the proposals approved by the parliament, one concerns economic support for families with children. However, Isabella stated that this measure is not sufficient and that broader, more structural interventions are needed. He also called for a review of existing policies to make them more effective in combating the decline in births.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Declining birth rates significantly impact cross-border workers employed in Ticino. With a shrinking population, there could be increased pressure on the job market and greater competition for job openings. Additionally, family support policies could influence decisions regarding relocation to Ticino.

### What to do
To address declining birth rates, Canton Ticino should consider a series of measures, including:
- Tax incentives for families with children
- Direct economic support for families
- Policies to balance work and family life
- Awareness campaigns on the importance of birth rates

### Conclusion
Claudio Isabella's criticism highlights the need for concrete actions to address declining birth rates in Ticino. It is important that the parliament and the cantonal government work together to develop an effective action plan and implement concrete measures to support families and encourage births. ## Analysis of Current Policies
The current policies of the Canton of Ticino to address low birth rates have been criticized for their lack of effectiveness. According to experts, a more integrated and coordinated approach is needed to tackle this complex issue.

### Comparison with Other Regions
Some Swiss and Italian regions have successfully implemented policies to combat low birth rates. For example, the Canton of Zurich has introduced tax incentives for families with children, while Lombardy has launched direct economic support programs. These experiences could be useful for Ticino in developing more effective policies.

### Future Scenarios
If no concrete measures are taken, low birth rates could have a significant impact on the economy and society of Ticino. A declining population could lead to a reduction in the workforce, increased pressure on public services, and greater dependence on immigration. Additionally, there could be a negative impact on the real estate market and educational services.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers employed in Ticino could be affected by low birth rates in various ways. A declining population could lead to increased competition for jobs, particularly in sectors with a strong presence of cross-border workers. Furthermore, family support policies could influence decisions about whether to move to Ticino or not.

### What to Do
To address low birth rates, the Canton of Ticino should consider a series of measures, including:
- Tax incentives for families with children
- Direct economic support for families
- Policies to reconcile work and family life
- Awareness campaigns on the importance of birth rates

### Conclusion
The analysis of current policies and the experiences of other regions shows that it is possible to address low birth rates with effective measures. It is important that the Canton of Ticino works to develop an integrated and coordinated action plan to support families and encourage births.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions to tackle low birth rates
To address low birth rates in Ticino, a concrete action plan with specific measures is necessary. Here are some actions that could be considered:

### Tax incentives
The Canton of Ticino could introduce tax incentives for families with children, such as tax reductions or tax deductions. These incentives could help support families economically and encourage births.

### Direct economic support
Another measure that could be considered is direct economic support for families with children. This could include family allowances, contributions for education, and other forms of financial assistance.

### Work-family reconciliation policies
To support families, the Canton of Ticino could introduce work-family reconciliation policies, such as extended parental leave, flexible working hours, and childcare services. These measures could help parents better balance work and family responsibilities.

### Awareness campaigns
Finally, the Canton of Ticino could launch awareness campaigns on the importance of birth rates and the support measures available. These campaigns could help inform families about the options available and encourage births.

### Conclusion
Addressing low birth rates in Ticino requires an integrated and coordinated approach. The measures described above could help support families and encourage births, contributing to a more prosperous future for the Canton of Ticino.

For more information on how to tackle low birth rates and the implications for cross-border workers, consult our [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/ticino/1921880/non-basta-riconoscere-il-problema-della-denatalita-bisogna-agire)*

item.title: AI and Hiring: New Challenges for the Ticino Market
item.description: The use of AI in recruitment raises concerns regarding discrimination and bias. An analysis of risks for candidates and companies in Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- AI is being used to optimize staff recruitment in Switzerland.
- Cases of incorrect exclusion and unsuitable profiles have been reported.
- Algorithms risk penalizing candidates with non-linear career paths.
- A collective approach to software regulation is necessary.

## Key facts
- What: Use of artificial intelligence in hiring
- When: Second quarter of 2026
- Where: Switzerland and Canton Ticino
- Who: Employers and human resources managers
- Source: Manpower quarterly report

Artificial intelligence has entered the Swiss labor market, bringing with it expectations of efficiency and new management complexities. According to the Manpower quarterly report for the second quarter of 2026, employers remain cautious in their hiring forecasts. In an attempt to attract talent and manage internal processes, companies are increasingly turning to AI-based software for candidate screening. This digital transition, however, is not without its critical issues. Human resources managers have begun reporting instances of the erroneous exclusion of potentially valid profiles, while some companies have reported receiving applications that do not align with actual operational needs. The problem often lies in how the systems are trained. Machine learning algorithms rely on vast sets of historical data. If these data reflect pre-existing biases or traditional career patterns, the software will tend to replicate those models, excluding those who do not fit standard criteria. The issue also directly concerns [Canton Ticino](nav:municipalities), where the integration of advanced technologies into selection processes is under observation to ensure transparency and fairness toward every candidate. The challenge for local businesses consists of balancing the automation of [recruitment](nav:jobs) processes with the need for a human evaluation capable of grasping the intrinsic value of each CV, avoiding the risk that technological efficiency turns into a barrier to entry for diverse professional profiles. The caution expressed by companies in the current period reflects the awareness that, although AI can speed up screening, the final quality of the choice remains a human responsibility, subject to variables that computer code cannot always interpret correctly, especially in a dynamic and varied labor market like the Swiss one. ### The Critical Impact on Female Careers

The issue of discriminatory effects linked to AI becomes particularly evident when examining female professional careers. Algorithms, trained on historical data, tend to favor linear and continuous career paths, a characteristic more frequently found in male profiles. Consequently, resumes with interruptions, part-time work periods, or career changes are often penalized by selection software. Bea Knecht, founder of Zattoo, highlighted how systems learn from the past: if the past does not adequately document the female presence, the software risks not recognizing it as existing. For Professor Solange Ghernaouti of the University of Lausanne, the issue is even deeper: algorithms facilitate the automation of discriminations, making them almost indisputable as they are integrated directly into the software. In this way, artificial intelligence acts as an amplifier of inequalities already present in the labor market, rather than correcting them. This dynamic also affects minorities, whose profiles may not align with the dominant models used to train selection systems. Companies operating in [Switzerland](nav:living-ch), including those active in the services and industry sectors in Ticino, must be aware of these limitations. Blindly relying on an algorithmic tool can lead to the loss of valuable talents and the creation of less diverse teams. It is necessary to question the ethics of the software used and the transparency of the selection criteria. According to experts, it is not enough to merely increase female presence in scientific sectors: models capable of proposing pluralistic and alternative visions to the current structure are needed. The concrete risk is that technology, born to improve efficiency, becomes an invisible but insurmountable barrier. The discussion therefore shifts to the governance of AI: who is responsible when an algorithm erroneously excludes a candidate? The answer cannot be delegated exclusively to technicians but requires a collective reflection involving HR, lawyers, and political scientists to ensure that innovation does not sacrifice social equity in favor of pure data processing speed.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Towards informed and regulated selection

Despite the risks, the perspective on artificial intelligence is not exclusively negative. Bea Knecht suggests that AI can be a truly egalitarian tool, accessible to anyone without requiring long periods of specific technical training. The key lies in understanding how the tool works: women, and more generally all workers, must learn to manage these technologies to improve their professional standing. Political scientist Anna Jobin highlights the urgency of a collective approach to regulation, arguing that decisions regarding AI must go beyond technical expertise. For companies in Ticino, the next step is to implement control systems that work alongside AI, ensuring constant human oversight. Before adopting automated solutions, it is advisable to verify the compliance of selection criteria and the transparency of the data used by the software provider. For candidates, however, it becomes essential to present a resume that, while unique, is readable by screening systems. Optimizing one's professional profile also means understanding how algorithmic filters interpret career breaks or unconventional experiences. Despite technological evolution, the human interview remains the decisive moment to assert one's real skills. For those looking for work or wishing to evaluate their contractual situation in a market influenced by these changes, the use of comparison tools is increasingly useful. Before facing selection interviews in companies that use automated systems, it is helpful to have a clear understanding of one's salary and contractual position. By using our [salary calculator](nav:calculator), it is possible to obtain a precise estimate of your remuneration based on your profile, helping you to navigate the challenges of a labor market in continuous technological evolution with greater awareness. The responsibility for an ethical and inclusive use of AI remains, ultimately, in the hands of the humans who design it, purchase it, and use it to decide people's professional futures. Vigilance is the first step in ensuring that innovation does not become an obstacle to fair professional progress.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lavoro-ed-economia/lia-nella-selezione-del-personale-tra-opportunit%c3%a0-e-rischi-di-discriminazione/91193289)*

item.title: Roberto Grassi is the new president of LIUC University
item.description: The Board of Directors of LIUC has appointed Roberto Grassi as the new president of the university in Castellanza.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Roberto Grassi is the new president of LIUC in Castellanza
- He led Confindustria Varese for six years
- He replaces Riccardo Comerio, president since 2019

## Key facts
- **What**: Appointment of the new president of LIUC
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Castellanza
- Who: Roberto Grassi
- Amount: Not specified

The Board of Directors of LIUC – Università Cattaneo has appointed Roberto Grassi as the new president of the university in Castellanza. The appointment took place during the meeting on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. Grassi, who led Confindustria Varese for six years before stepping down in May 2025, takes over from Riccardo Comerio, who has been president since 2019 and recently became president of ITS INCOM Academy. Comerio will remain on the Board of Directors as a counselor: the board thanked him for his contribution to strengthening the university's brand in the national and international academic landscape, and for affirming its role as a competitive lever for local businesses. For Confindustria Varese, the reference shareholder and historical soul of LIUC, this transition is part of a process of alternating leadership aimed at ensuring a continuous influx of new ideas and perspectives, while maintaining a strong link between the university and the business community that originated it. The Board of Directors wished the new president, Roberto Grassi, to lead LIUC towards a future of quality and innovation, always at the service of education and the realization of young people.

### Roberto Grassi's background
Roberto Grassi has a long entrepreneurial background. He led Confindustria Varese for six years, stepping down in May 2025. His appointment as president of LIUC is an important recognition of his commitment to the business and academic worlds. Founded in 1991, LIUC is a reference point for higher education in Lombardy, with a strong connection to the world of work and business.

### The role of Riccardo Comerio
Riccardo Comerio, who has led LIUC since 2019, recently took over as president of ITS INCOM Academy. His contribution has been fundamental for strengthening the university's brand and affirming its role as a competitive lever for local businesses. Comerio will remain on the Board of Directors as a counselor, continuing to contribute to the university's growth.

### Future prospects for LIUC
With the appointment of Roberto Grassi, LIUC looks to the future with new prospects. The Board of Directors expressed the wish that Grassi will lead the university towards a future of quality and innovation, always at the service of education and the realization of young people. LIUC continues to be a reference point for higher education in Lombardy, with a strong connection to the world of work and business. ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers
The appointment of Roberto Grassi as the new president of LIUC in Castellanza represents a significant change for the university and the local business community. For cross-border workers who commute to Lombardy from Ticino, this appointment could have several implications, particularly in terms of educational opportunities and collaboration between businesses in the two regions.

### Educational opportunities for cross-border workers
LIUC is known for its strong ties to the working world and businesses. Grassi's appointment, given his extensive experience in the business sector, could lead to new educational opportunities for cross-border workers. Specifically, new courses and training programs could be developed to meet the specific needs of transborder workers, providing them with tools to improve their skills and enhance their career opportunities.

### Collaboration between Lombard and Ticinese businesses
Another important aspect is the collaboration between Lombard and Ticinese businesses. LIUC has always had a strong connection with the Lombard business world, and Grassi's appointment could further strengthen this collaboration. This could lead to new job and business opportunities for cross-border workers, who could benefit from greater integration between the two regions.

### Innovation and quality of education
The Board of Directors has expressed the hope that Grassi will guide LIUC towards a future of quality and innovation. This could mean greater investment in advanced educational technologies and the creation of new study programs that meet the needs of the job market. For cross-border workers, this could mean access to high-quality education that better prepares them for the challenges of the transborder job market.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers interested in LIUC
For cross-border workers who are interested in attending LIUC or collaborating with the university, there are several steps to take. First, it's important to stay updated on new educational opportunities that may be offered. LIUC might announce new courses or training programs specifically for cross-border workers, and it will be important to be ready to seize these opportunities.

### Concrete steps for cross-border workers
1. **Learn about new opportunities**: Visit the LIUC website and subscribe to newsletters to receive updates on new educational initiatives.
2. Contact the orientation office: The orientation office at LIUC can provide detailed information about available courses and how to enroll.
3. Attend events and conferences: LIUC regularly organizes events and conferences that can be useful for cross-border workers. Participating in these events can be a great opportunity to network and discover new opportunities.
4. Use support services: LIUC offers a range of support services for students, including career counseling, tutoring, and psychological support. These services can be particularly useful for cross-border workers facing the challenges of living and working in two different countries.

### Useful tools for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who want to explore the opportunities offered by LIUC, there are several useful tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help understand the financial implications of attending a training course in Lombardy. Additionally, the [municipality map](nav:border-map) can be useful for finding information about services available in different municipalities in the region.

### Conclusion
The appointment of Roberto Grassi as the new president of LIUC in Castellanza represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers who want to improve their skills and increase their career opportunities. Staying updated on new educational initiatives and using the support services offered by LIUC can be fundamental steps to make the most of these opportunities.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/roberto-grassi-e-il-nuovo-presidente-delluniversita-liuc-di-castellanza/2560879/)*

item.title: A2 traffic chaos: over an hour delays between Chiasso and Lugano
item.description: Nightmare morning on the A2 northbound with delays up to one hour and forty minutes. Accidents and police checks complicate traffic
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Delays up to 1h40 on the A2 northbound
- Lane reduction before San Nicolao
- Accidents at Vedeggio-Cassarate and police checks

## Key facts
- **What**: Lane reduction and accidents
- When: Morning of April 30, 2026
- Where: Between Chiasso and Lugano Nord
- Who: Police and road assistance
- Impact: Delays up to 1h40

The morning of April 30, 2026, was a nightmare for drivers heading north on the A2. The queue began as early as 6:00 AM, right after Mendrisio, due to a lane reduction before the San Nicolao tunnel. The TCS confirmed disruptions between Chiasso and Lugano Nord, with delays of up to one hour and forty minutes. The primary cause of the significant delay was the reduction of lanes from two to one.

Before the Melide dam bridge, a police patrol checked the road surface for possible debris or objects on the carriageway. A scooter was towed away by road assistance, and absorbent material for polluting liquids was visible on the asphalt. The queue towards Lugano, which started earlier than usual, extended from Chiasso with the onset of rush hour. Traffic was also congested on the cantonal road, starting from Chiasso heading north, in a morning made even more complicated by unfavorable weather.

An accident also occurred at the Vedeggio-Cassarate roundabout, with a scooter on the ground and a police patrol managing the traffic. For drivers, it was a Thursday morning to forget.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who use the A2 motorway daily to commute to Switzerland had to face significant delays. This type of situation can directly impact punctuality at work, with possible consequences on working hours and performance. Additionally, prolonged delays can affect the quality of life of cross-border workers, increasing stress and reducing leisure time.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to similar situations in the past, the delays recorded this morning are among the longest in recent years. The reduction of lanes and the presence of accidents contributed to creating a traffic jam that exceeded two hours of waiting. This type of situation is particularly critical for cross-border workers who must adhere to fixed working hours and often use the motorway for daily commutes. ## Analysis of the situation

The traffic situation on the A2 northbound has highlighted some structural issues that require attention. The narrowing to one lane before the San Nicolao tunnel has been the main trigger for delays. This type of problem is recurrent and requires targeted interventions to improve traffic flow.

### Possible solutions

1. **Increase in lanes**: A structural intervention to increase the number of lanes could reduce waiting times in case of accidents or roadworks.
2. Improved signage: More effective signage could help manage traffic better in case of narrowings or accidents.
3. Strengthening of rescue services: Faster intervention by rescue services could reduce lane closure times and improve traffic flow.

### Comparison with other motorways

Compared to other Swiss motorways, the A2 northbound has a greater tendency to congestion. This is due to the presence of tunnels, bridges and roundabouts that can create critical points in case of accidents or roadworks. However, other motorways such as the A1 or the A13 present similar problems, especially in correspondence with large cities or complex road nodes.

### Impact on local traffic

The congested traffic on the A2 has had a significant impact on local traffic. The cantonal road, which connects Chiasso to Lugano Nord, has registered an increase in traffic and travel times. This has caused further inconvenience for drivers and increased the risk of accidents.

### Future scenarios

In the event of structural interventions, such as increasing the number of lanes or improving signage, a reduction in waiting times and greater traffic fluidity could be observed. However, without targeted interventions, the situation could worsen, especially with the increase in traffic due to demographic growth and the increase in the number of cross-border workers.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do in case of delays

In case of prolonged delays on the A2, it is important to follow some guidelines to minimize inconveniences:

1. **Get real-time information**: Use apps and websites that provide updated traffic information, such as the TCS website or navigation apps.
2. Plan alternative routes: Knowing alternative routes can be useful in case of lane closures or accidents.
3. Leave earlier: In case of expected delays, leaving with a larger time margin can help avoid being late for work.
4. Contact your employer: Informing your employer in case of significant delays can help manage the situation better.

### Useful Tools

To plan your trips better, you can use tools like the [traffic calculator](nav:traffic-history) or the [route comparator](nav:border-map). These tools can provide useful information on travel times and possible delays.

### Conclusion

The traffic situation on the A2 heading north has highlighted the need for targeted interventions to improve traffic flow. Cross-border workers who use the highway daily must be prepared for possible delays and adopt strategies to minimize inconveniences. Using planning tools and getting real-time information can make a difference in managing traffic and the quality of daily life.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/cronaca/1922005/traffico-in-tilt-sulla-a2-e-ritardi-oltre-l-ora)*

item.title: Demographic decline alarms Ticino's future
item.description: Low birth rate and aging population concern the Canton of Ticino. Parliamentary question for a cantonal strategy.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Ticino at risk of demographic glaciation
- Birth rate below replacement level
- Elderly population on the rise

## Key facts
- **What**: Possible demographic glaciation
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Canton of Ticino
- Who: Claudio Isabella, Alessandro Corti, Il Centro Group
- Amount: Not specified

The Canton of Ticino is facing a potential demographic emergency. The alarm was raised by Claudio Isabella and Alessandro Corti, along with the Il Centro Group and the young members of Il Centro, through an interrogation presented on April 28, 2026. The phenomenon, described as a possible 'demographic glaciation,' is characterized by a birth rate consistently below the replacement level and a progressive increase in the elderly population in need of care.

Public data indicates a scenario that, in the medium term, risks putting pressure on the sustainability of the Canton's social, pension, and economic systems. According to the promoters, ignoring the problem would mean intervening too late, when the effects will already be consolidated. In recent months, the Grand Council has discussed the so-called 'poker on birth rates,' only partially accepting it. The proposals put forward were not considered adequate by the State Council.

However, concerns are growing from various sectors: economic associations, trade unions, schools, and research bodies describe the situation as critical or even dramatic. According to the signatories, the issue cannot be addressed in a fragmented way. 'A clear political management is needed, with a defined departmental responsibility and a cantonal strategy capable of integrating family policies, work, training, taxation, and territorial attractiveness.'

The interrogation therefore asks the State Council to clarify which Department will be responsible for the demographic dossier, with what timelines and objectives, and whether monitoring measures and new statistical tools are planned. It also seeks a position on the political priority of the issue, the available resources, and the possible development of an organic action plan.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The possible demographic glaciation could have significant repercussions for cross-border workers employed in Ticino. A decline in the active population could increase pressure on the labor market, making it harder to find employment. Moreover, an increase in the elderly population could lead to a greater demand for healthcare and pension services, with possible impacts on welfare policies and available resources.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this interrogation, the issue of birth rates in Ticino had been addressed in a partial and fragmented manner. The 'poker on birth rates' discussed by the Grand Council represented an attempt to tackle the problem, but the proposals put forward were not considered sufficient. The interrogation presented by Isabella and Corti represents a significant step forward, as it calls for an integrated cantonal strategy and clear departmental responsibility.

### Future scenarios

If adequate measures are not taken, the Canton of Ticino could face a demographic crisis with serious consequences for the economy and the pension system. A decline in the active population could lead to a reduction in tax revenues and increased pressure on public services. Furthermore, an aging population could increase pension and healthcare costs, making it harder to sustain the system.

For cross-border workers, this could mean greater competition for jobs and a possible reduction in employment opportunities. Additionally, an increase in demand for healthcare and pension services could lead to higher taxes or a reduction in available services.

### Procedure for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers employed in Ticino should closely monitor the developments of this parliamentary interrogation. It is important to stay informed about any measures that the State Council might adopt to address demographic glaciation. Additionally, cross-border workers should consider participating in local initiatives that promote birth rates and the attraction of new residents to the Canton.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who wish to delve deeper into the issue, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used to compare wages in Ticino and Italy. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful for evaluating different residence and work options. ### Analysis of Practical Implications

The possible demographic glaciazione in Ticino could have several practical implications for cross-border workers. A decline in the active population could lead to increased competition for jobs, making it harder to find employment. Additionally, an increase in the elderly population could raise the demand for healthcare and pension services, with potential impacts on welfare policies and available resources.

### Comparison with Other Regions

Ticino is not the only region facing a demographic crisis. Other regions in Switzerland and Italy are also dealing with similar problems, with declining birth rates and an aging population. However, the situation in Ticino is particularly critical due to its geographical position and dependence on the cross-border economy.

### Future Scenarios for Cross-Border Workers

If adequate measures are not taken, cross-border workers could face a series of challenges. A decline in the active population could lead to a reduction in employment opportunities and increased competition for jobs. Furthermore, an increase in demand for healthcare and pension services could lead to higher taxes or a reduction in available services.

### Mitigation Measures

To address the demographic glaciazione, the Canton of Ticino could adopt various measures. These include incentives for families with children, territorial attractiveness policies to attract new residents, and investments in training and education. Additionally, it may be necessary to reform the pension system to ensure long-term sustainability.

### Implications for Welfare Policies

An increase in the elderly population could have a significant impact on welfare policies in Ticino. It may be necessary to increase resources allocated to healthcare and pension services, with potential consequences for public finances. Additionally, it may be necessary to reform the social assistance system to ensure that it is sustainable in the long term.

### Useful Tools for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers in Ticino can use various tools to address the challenges related to demographic glaciazione. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be useful for comparing salaries in Ticino and Italy. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help evaluate different residence and work options.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers employed in Ticino can adopt various measures to address the challenges related to demographic stagnation. First and foremost, it is important to closely monitor the developments of cantonal policies and participate in local initiatives that promote birth rates and the attraction of new residents. Additionally, cross-border workers can consider the opportunity to invest in training and education to improve their skills and increase employment opportunities.

### Procedure to obtain updated information

To stay informed about cantonal policies and local initiatives, cross-border workers can regularly consult the website of [Canton Ticino](https://www.ti.ch). Additionally, they can participate in meetings and conferences organized by economic associations and trade unions to discuss demographic challenges and possible solutions.

### Useful tools for financial planning

Cross-border workers can use various tools to plan their finances and address the challenges related to demographic stagnation. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be useful for comparing salaries in Ticino and Italy. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help evaluate different residence and work options.

### Deadlines and required documents

Cross-border workers must ensure they have all the necessary documents to work and reside in Ticino. These include the residence permit, work contract, and tax documents. It is important to keep all documents up-to-date and comply with the deadlines set by the cantonal authorities.

### Support and assistance

Cross-border workers can turn to various organizations for support and assistance. For example, the [Employment Office](https://www.ti.ch/lavoro) can provide information on job opportunities and welfare policies. Additionally, economic associations and trade unions can offer legal and financial support.

### Conclusion

The possible demographic stagnation in Ticino represents a significant challenge for cross-border workers and the cantonal economy. However, by adopting adequate measures and using the available tools, cross-border workers can address these challenges and continue to work and live in Ticino. It is important to stay informed, participate in local initiatives, and plan finances to ensure a secure and prosperous future.
